title: holcus sorghum var. transiens
intro: Holcus sorghum var. transiens, also known as broomcorn or sorghum, is a plant species that belongs to the grass family and is native to Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Traditionally, broomcorn has been prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as being used in tincture and decoction forms, to leverage its medicinal properties and health benefits.
